rationale: PLTR is the one new midday flow signal strong enough to justify using the last portfolio slot: bullish call flow has a 100 composite score, 18.2x volume/open interest, full ask-side aggression, 8.0x call/put notional, and about $68.7M notional in the 128-strike calls. Technically, the stock is trying to reclaim momentum by trading just above its 20-day SMA, though it remains below the 50-day, so size should stay conservative. The entry offers asymmetric follow-through potential if the sweep marks institutional demand, with a tightened stop to control risk.

flow_signal.rationale: Dominant aggressive call buying (call/put notional 8.0x); treated as a long entry trigger on PLTR.

market_context.summary: Market sentiment appears stable with the VIX at 16.26, indicating a normal regime and a slight decrease in volatility. Notable single-name setups include Apple (AAPL), which is trading above its 50-day moving average and showing resilience despite recent headlines, while AMD (AMD) remains strong above its 50-day SMA but has pulled back from recent highs. Amazon (AMZN) is underperforming, trading below its 50-day SMA, suggesting potential weakness. Hims & Hers (HIMS) is gaining attention with a high RSI of 70.6 and trading above its moving averages, indicating bullish momentum. Meanwhile, Robinhood (HOOD) is also showing strength with a solid upward trend and a high RSI, reflecting positive investor sentiment.
